item.title: Modern design patterns
item.description: Table of contents Builder Fluent interface Facets Factory method Factory Inner factory Factory vs Builder Prototype Singleton Singleton problems Monostate Multiton Adapter Bridge PIMPL as a Bridge implementation Composite Array backed properties Decorator Proxy Proxy vs Decorator Command CQS - Command Query Separation Interpreter Iterator Mediator Strategy (policy) Template method Memento (token) Observer State Visitor std::visit First of all, modern design pattern follows all the software best practices (i.

item.title: C++17
item.description: Table of contents Language elements Nested namespaces Variable declaration in control statements constexpr if Structured bindings Language elements Nested namespaces Instead of nesting the namespaces namespace A { namespace B { namespace C { // some code here } } } we can explicitly nest them namespace A::B::C { // some code here } Variable declaration in control statements Ability to declare variable that is used inside the control statement body.

item.title: C++14
item.description: Table of contents Language elements tuples getters improvements Generic lambdas && init capture Language elements tuples getters improvements ability to access element by type: std::get<double>(tuple) if more than 1 element of this type - compiler error Generic lambdas && init capture Allow to use auto as parameter type [](const auto& lhs, const auto& rhs) { return lhs.length() < rhs.length(); } Ability to init variable when capturing to lambda

item.title: QML
item.description: Table of contents General concepts Item Signal / slots Property system Tips How to add an image How to add new component? How to include Javascript file? How to use persistance? Model View Delegate Repeaters XmlModelList Transforms / Animations / States & Transitions Transforms Animations States / transitions Controls / Widgets Mouse Area Group box ComboBox BusyIndicator General concepts Item Item is an abstract, invisible type, used to group objects, all QML objects inherits from it.

item.title: Git tips & tricks
item.description: Table of contents Fetch branch (not current) from remote repo (prepare for rebase): Force push only your current branch: Rename local and remote branch Rebase onto Automatically push without setting remote branch manually Set mergetool and difftool Remove untracked files: Reset one merge result (single file) Change all commits in repository to new author Fetch branch (not current) from remote repo (prepare for rebase): git fetch origin release:release Force push only your current branch: git push origin +youBranchName Rename local and remote branch # Rename branch locally git branch -m old_branch new_branch # Delete the old branch git push origin :old_branch # Push the new branch, set local branch to track the new remote git push --set-upstream origin new_branch Rebase onto Providing we have:

item.title: Refactoring legacy code
item.description: Note: below you can find my jottings based on Kate Gregory course Table of contents Introduction How much time dedicate to refactoring? Refactoring process Use compiler - it’s you friend Use correct C++ as a C++ Use new C++ features Use standard library Reorganizing code Introduction Refactoring legacy code generally means transforming code to be: more expressive, more readable, simpler.
